Cause of Death: Electrocution from live wire and energized light pole in floodwater
Notes: Andrew Pasek, 25, an Eagle Scout, was electrocuted while wading through Harvey floodwater to rescue his sister's cat. His ankle hit a submerged live wire (metal plates from prior surgery attracted the current), and he grabbed an energized light pole for support. His last words: 'Don't touch me, I'm dying.' He lay in the water 1.5 hours before power could be cut. The cat (Momo) was eventually rescued. Family sued CenterPoint Energy.

On May 14, 2018, a man's grandson was shocked at Lake Taneycomo, Branson, Missouri. The cause was faulty electrical wiring three docks away from the victim's location, demonstrating how far stray voltage can travel through water.

On Saturday, June 2, 2018, shortly after 3 p.m., a 17-year-old female from Franklin County was shocked at a dock in the Gills Creek area of Smith Mountain Lake, Virginia. She was holding a boat lift cable with her hand while her foot was in the water, becoming the path of least resistance. She fell into the lake after being shocked and was pulled from the water by another teen. She was transferred to Carilion Roanoke Memorial Hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. The incident prompted Smith Mountain Lake Marine Volunteer Fire Rescue to offer free stray voltage checks; an alarming 96% of nearly 200 docks checked had some stray voltage detected.

On Monday, June 11, 2018, swimmers felt electric shock at Camelot Cove, Camdenton, MO on Lake of the Ozarks. A bare energized wire was found dangling in the water from a neighbor's dock. The incident was described as potentially fatal — the wiring problem 'could have killed several people who were swimming in the Lake.' Discovery occurred before anyone was seriously injured.

Facility Name: Private dock near J Road, 5 Mile Marker
Notes: On July 3, 2018, at 2 p.m., a woman was shocked on a metal dock ladder at Rocky Mount near the 5 Mile Marker on Lake of the Ozarks, MO. She reported arcing between the ladder and the rings on her hand while exiting the water. Several small children also reported feeling electrical sensations in the water. Rocky Mount Fire Protection District responded and measured AC voltage levels ranging from 3.5 to 15 volts — levels Fire Chief Kevin Hurtibise identified as 'life threatening.' Investigation revealed a faulty water heater was sending voltage through the ground wire to the dock's electrical system. The fire department required inspection before dock power could be reconnected.

On July 4, 2018, Shelly Colucci felt a tingling sensation in her feet while entering her family's pool in Canfield, Ohio. She exited, tested the water with her hand and felt the same tingle, recognized she was being shocked, and immediately ordered her son out of the pool. Ohio Edison technicians confirmed stray voltage in the water. The family subsequently installed a Shock Alert detection device. Shelly Colucci publicly warned other pool owners to safeguard their pools.

Wesley Seeley, 23, was leaning against a bollard light along Bricktown Canal when it gave way, sending him into the water. He grabbed the bollard to get out and was electrocuted. Brandon Gann jumped in to save him and was also severely shocked. 18 loose bollards were found. Bollards had been scheduled for removal months earlier. Libra Electric found 75% liable.

Facility Name: Dock area at end of Appleton Street
Notes: On November 4, 2018, a woman was walking her 13-year-old black Labrador retriever near Paugus Bay (Lake Winnipesaukee) at the end of Appleton Street in Laconia, NH. The dog escaped and jumped into the water, where it was electrocuted. The owner suffered a powerful electric shock while attempting to rescue the dog and was transported to hospital for evaluation. Laconia Fire Department confirmed electrical current in the water. Eversource shut off power to docks and a light pole and determined the hazard was caused by 'customer-owned equipment.' The dog's body was recovered after power was disconnected.

Facility Name: Metal pedestrian bridge over Weyland Canal, Dixon Avenue West
Notes: Jacob Hourmouzus (17) and Jacob Schneider (17), both Dixon High School students, were electrocuted after jumping into an irrigation canal to rescue a dog. When they grabbed the metal bridge to get out, they were paralyzed by the current. Schneider's cousin Kelby Holland jumped in and knocked them free. The dog survived. The lawsuit alleged district workers improperly bypassed a circuit breaker.

Facility Name: Universal Studios Volcano Bay water park
Notes: On June 2, 2019, numerous staff and guests were shocked at Universal Studios Volcano Bay water park in Orlando, FL. Guests reported sensations described as 'zapping,' 'bee stings all over their legs,' and numbness radiating through their bodies. Shocks began shortly after park opening and continued for hours before the park closed early. Five lifeguards were hospitalized and released same day. OSHA investigation found the cause was a construction defect: a grounding rod inadvertently driven through an electrical conduit during construction, damaging wiring and energizing wet surfaces and water throughout the park. OSHA issued no fines, concluding Universal was unaware of the defect. At least one guest (April Carlino) filed a lawsuit.

Cause of Death: Electrocution from pool light under repair
Notes: McKenzie Kinley, 9, was electrocuted while swimming at her father's home in Citrus Heights. She touched a live wire on an unsealed recessed pool light that was under repair. Four other children in the pool were unharmed. The home was built in the late 1950s. Parents believed the light was disconnected from power. Father Cliff Kinley spoke out about pool electrical safety.

Hunter Morrison, 19, of Fannett TX, was electrocuted while trying to move his horse during Tropical Storm Imelda flooding. First confirmed death from Imelda in Jefferson County. The source of the electricity was never publicly identified. A lightning storm was occurring simultaneously. Jefferson County received 40+ inches of rain in 72 hours.

Facility Name: Private dock (non-electrified; stray voltage from neighbor's dock)
Notes: On May 11, 2020, Meredith Defoe was shocked at her dock on Lewis Smith Lake, Alabama, despite her dock having no electricity installed. Stray voltage was coming from her neighbor's dock. She stated 'It paralyzed me from my feet all the way up.' She attempted to swim toward shore but was shocked more severely when she grabbed a dock cable. Her husband rescued her by extending a pole for her to grab. She stated she would have died without his intervention. Following the incident, she installed a Dock Lifeguard electrical detection device and became an advocate for dock electrical safety.

Brothers Timothy Miller (53) and Michael Miller (50) died at Scorpion Bay Marina on Lake Pleasant. Timothy jumped in around 6:30 PM and was immediately overcome. Michael jumped in to rescue him. Timothy's girlfriend also entered and suffered burn marks. Investigation found the victims' own boat had an improper electrical modification that bypassed safety features, discharging electricity around the swim platform. Marina systems were not at fault.

Cause of Death: Electrocution from exposed pool light wires
Notes: Khaleel Marcos Reynolds, 15, was electrocuted in the North Villa Inn motel pool in Houston. Someone had removed an interior pool light, leaving wires exposed. The pool had failed inspections in July and December 2018 and was never permitted to operate. The family lived at the motel where his mother worked; she was reportedly fired and evicted after the death. Both parents filed wrongful death lawsuits.

Cause of Death: Electrocution from dryer in flooded laundry room
Notes: Mark Mixon, 65, was electrocuted while sandbagging his Bradenton Beach home during Tropical Storm Eta. He stepped into the flooded laundry room containing approximately 4 inches of water and a plugged-in dryer. A firefighter was also injured attempting to reach the scene. EMS had to wait for FPL to cut power. Not classic dock/marina ESD — storm flooding/indoor electrocution.

Trevor Cate, 18, a 2021 Clyde High School graduate, was electrocuted at Clyde Lake while swimming and towing a disabled sailboat. The sailboat struck a power line, which broke and fell into the water. Lake officials had to cut power before rescuers could reach him. He was transported to Hendrick Medical Center and died June 3.

Cause of Death: Electrocution from submerged extension cord in floodwater
Facility Name: 3700 block of North Palm Leaf Circle, La Homa
Notes: Gustavo Delgado, 14, was electrocuted while walking through a flooded street in the La Homa area near Mission, TX. He stepped into a puddle containing a submerged extension cord that ran from a house to a trailer home. He was reportedly attempting to unplug the cord. Severe thunderstorms had caused flooding in the area. Not classic dock/marina ESD — extension cord in floodwater.

James DeAngelo, 23, of Venetia PA, was found unresponsive while swimming near a docked boat at Beach Club Marina in New Eagle on the Monongahela River around 7:30 PM. A friend who jumped in to search 'felt horribly shocked.' Three others in the area also suffered shocks, strongly supporting ESD diagnosis. Initial 911 call reported electrical current in the water.

Facility Name: Prospect Yacht Club / Drift Bar, 1900 Victory Lane
Notes: William Keith Elkins, 58, a self-employed commercial diver, was electrocuted while working underwater at Prospect Yacht Club preparing barges for Thunder Over Louisville. Kentucky issued a stop work order 2 days before his death due to lack of building permits. State inspectors later found 33 electrical violations with electricity leaking at 4+ amps. The fire marshal shut down the yacht club indefinitely. OSHA declined to investigate (independent contractor). Yacht club's attorney disputed the electrocution finding.

Cause of Death: Electrocution — mobile boat lift contacted overhead power lines
Facility Name: Marina (location not published in OSHA summary)
Notes: At 3:45 PM on August 3, 2022, a marina employee operating a mobile boat lift to move boats into slips was electrocuted when the lift contacted overhead power lines. He fell from the operator's cab and was pronounced dead at the scene. NAICS 713930 (Marinas). The victim was not in or near water — this is an overhead power line contact, not ESD by mechanism. However, it documents OSHA investigation of a marina-setting fatality.

Cause of Death: Electrocution — stepped in standing water with live wire
Facility Name: Russell Woods Apartments, 11500 block of LaSalle Street
Notes: PROBABLE PHANTOM DUPLICATE. This entry (FI-2022-001, dated Aug 16, 2022) has word-for-word identical description to FI-2023-001 (dated Feb 7, 2023). All evidence points to a single incident: the death of James Stanley, 62, on February 7, 2023, at Russell Woods Apartments in Detroit. No news coverage exists for any similar Detroit basement electrocution in August 2022. See ESD-2023-02-07-1 for the actual incident record.

Cause of Death: Electrocution — stepped in standing water with live wire while snaking drain
Facility Name: Russell Woods Apartments, 11500 block of LaSalle Street at Webb
Notes: James Stanley, 62, of Oakland County (Pontiac area), was electrocuted at 6:17 PM while snaking a drain in the flooded basement of Russell Woods Apartments on Detroit's west side. He stepped in standing water containing a live wire. The entire building was electrically charged through 3-4 feet of flood water. Fire crews had to wait for DTE Energy to shut off power. His wife Sherri: 'the 29 years we were married were the best years.' Not classic dock/marina ESD — indoor flooded basement.

Cause of Death: Electrocution — removed from life support July 30, 2023
Facility Name: Wright Island Marina (Drake Ave. Marina, Inc.)
Notes: Dr. Kole Shtjefni Nikprelaj, 24, of Bedford NY, and his girlfriend Dila Bujaj had just finished jet-skiing at Wright Island Marina in New Rochelle when both were electrocuted by a live wire in the water near the dock/boat ramp. Both were found unconscious in the water by a bystander and pulled out. Bujaj survived; Nikprelaj was placed on life support and died July 30, 2023, approximately three weeks after the incident. Fire officials confirmed the boat ramp had become electrified.

Cause of Death: Freshwater drowning possibly secondary to electrocution
Facility Name: Private dock, Mid Lake Heights Drive
Notes: Dr. Marshall L. Meadors III, 65, of Anderson SC, was found dead in the water at Lake Hartwell near Mid Lake Heights Drive. He went missing Monday evening; authorities were called around 8 PM. Investigators found exposed electrical wires on an electrical box providing power to the dock. The Anderson County Coroner ruled the preliminary cause of death as freshwater drowning possibly secondary to electrocution. An autopsy was scheduled.

Cause of Death: Electric shock drowning — electrocuted upon entering water near energized dock
Facility Name: Private dock near Dove Trail, Lanier Beach South Road area
Notes: Thomas 'Shep' Milner, 24, jumped off his family's dock into Lake Lanier near Dove Trail in Forsyth County on July 27, 2023. He was heard screaming for help shortly after entering the water. A neighbor jumped in to rescue him but immediately felt a burning sensation he recognized as electrical shock. The neighbor swam ashore, turned off the dock power box, then re-entered to pull Milner out. Milner's uncle administered CPR until first responders arrived. Milner was transported to Northside Forsyth Hospital and died July 28. Georgia DNR confirmed electricity came from a nearby power box. His mother Martha Milner said the dock was less than 3 years old and outfitted by a licensed electrician.

Facility Name: Harbourside Place outdoor shopping center fountain, 149 Soundings Ave
Notes: Nathaniel 'Nate' Davenport, 45, a Navy veteran and father of four, was electrocuted at the Harbourside Place fountain in Jupiter, FL on October 22, 2023 at approximately 3:33 PM. Three of his children entered the interactive fountain/splash pad; two boys appeared face-down and distressed. Davenport jumped in to rescue them and immediately collapsed from electrocution. Bystanders located an emergency power shutoff. Eight people total required medical attention including three Davenport children and at least one other adult. Davenport was transported to Jupiter Medical Center and pronounced dead approximately one hour later. A prior incident on July 10, 2023, had shocked 15+ people at the same fountain (witness photographed visible wire); a Harbourside employee dismissed the concern and the fountain remained operational.

Cause of Death: Electric shock drowning — stray voltage from submerged boat lift (116+ volts)
Facility Name: Private dock, Lee Drive, Craddock Creek area
Notes: Jesse Cyrus Hamric, 18, from Clark/Steamboat Springs CO, was electrocuted at 3:44 AM on July 4, 2024, at a private dock on Smith Mountain Lake. A submerged boat lift (20+ years old) was producing 116+ volts. Two friends who jumped in to help were also shocked. First documented ESD death at Smith Mountain Lake. Retired firefighter Neil Harrington had tested 200+ SML docks and found stray voltage at nearly all. New dock electrical codes adopted by three counties. The Jesse Cyrus Hamric Foundation for Courage and Love was established. GoFundMe raised $111K+.

Cause of Death: Electrocution from metal dock ladder (under investigation)
Notes: A 13-year-old boy from Fulton County GA (Atlanta metro) was electrocuted trying to exit the water via a metal dock ladder at Blue Ridge Lake. Two boys attempting rescue and a woman were also shocked. Fannin County Coroner Becky Callihan is investigating. The victim was a certified scuba diver, making accidental drowning unlikely. Numerous electrical deficiencies found at the dock. Limited news coverage; primary local article is paywalled.

Facility Name: Marina Shores at Dune Harbor, D dock
Notes: Gabriel Gonzalez, 21, of Hebron IN, was electrocuted at Marina Shores on Burns Waterway. He dove in to retrieve a cooler that fell from a boat (slip holder told him to let it go). He felt a 'tingling sensation' before going under. His brother Francis (23) jumped in and was also overcome; a bystander threw a flotation device. Gabriel's body was recovered ~39 minutes later. Autopsy: drowning secondary to electrocution. A similar incident had occurred at the same marina just 4 days earlier. Marina lacked required NEC warning signs.
